reason: The text describes a situation where the people of Jerusalem faced threats and conspiracies from their adversaries, such as Sanballat and Tobiah, who were angered by the rebuilding of the walls. The people had to set up defenses and remain vigilant against potential attacks. While there is no explicit mention of a significant man-made, natural, or supernatural destruction by Yahweh affecting the general public, the text does portray a tense situation where the people relied on their faith in God for protection and guidance in the face of adversity.
